media.alt: A broad and detail version of the same image in two spheres. In the broad view, a panoramic view of a town in a valley surrounded by hills and mountains. There are numerous buildings, both large and small, with roads lined with trees. In the foreground, three figures stand on rock formations overlooking the town. The landscape is dotted with additional trees, and there is a mix of open fields and structures. In the detail image, two men standing on a rocky outcrop, one pointing, with a few houses and trees in the background. On an adjacent rock, a person sits under an umbrella next to an easel. More houses and a path are visible in the distance.

text.headline: Make, Believe Timeline
text.text: Public History Project hosted by the Center for Digital Inquiry and Learning at the University of Idaho Library.

media.alt: A newspaper page titled "Algemeen Handelsblad van Zaterdag 31 Januari 1931." The main headline reads "Spokane, The Playground of the Nation." An article discusses the city of Spokane, mentioning aspects like its location, development, and banking industry. A photograph shows a busy street in Spokane with cars and pedestrians. Additional smaller articles cover various topics, including a theater fire and a political overview. "Een straat in Spokane." is captioned below the photograph.
media.caption: Spokane, the Playground of the Nation

text.headline: Spokane, the Playground of the Nation
text.text: Article in the Amsterdam-based newspaper, Algemeen Handelsblad, describing the history of Dutch investment in Spokane, WA.

media.alt: A scene of destruction with debris scattered across the ground. In the background are several tents, one with a sign that reads "U.S. GOVT. CLOTHING." A man on horseback is visible near some wooden structures and piles of rocks. The background shows the ruins of buildings. The text "Looking N. From R. Road Ave" is visible in the foreground.
media.caption: Spokane Fire, 1889 (#14)

text.headline: Spokane Fire, 1889 (#14)
text.text: Looking north from Railroad Avenue after the fire.

media.alt: A map depicting a town layout with streets labeled, including Broadway, Bridge St., W. Front St., W. Main St., and W. Riverside Ave. A red shaded area highlights a section of the map, encompassing multiple blocks. The map includes water channels labeled North Channel, Middle Channel, and South Channel, as well as Big Island and sections of land. The title reads "The Spokane Fire" with additional text about Spokane's building blocks.
media.caption: Spokane Fire, 1889 (#24)

text.headline: Spokane Fire, 1889 (#24)
text.text: Map showing boundaries of the August 4, 1889 fire. Map owned and drawn by Robert Hyslop, 1982.

media.alt: A detailed street map showing a grid layout with highlighted sections in red. Key street names include Riverside Avenue, Sprague Avenue, Main Avenue, and Trent Avenue. The Spokane River is marked, and several train yards and railway lines are shown. The map contains various numbered plots, with some areas shaded in red, likely indicating significant areas or properties.
media.caption: County Deed Records, 1893–97

text.headline: County Deed Records, 1893–97
text.text: Hypotheekbank property ownership noted on business district map of Spokane. From 1925 map, Spokane City Engineer.

media.alt: A decorative tin featuring an illustration of a windmill by a waterway under a moonlit sky. The border is adorned with intricate floral patterns. Text at the bottom reads "Cocoamill de Zaanstroom" with the numbers 18 and 43 on either side.
media.caption: Dutch Cocoa Powder Tin

text.headline: Dutch Cocoa Powder Tin
text.text: Decorative tin of Dutch cocoa powder housed in Sloane's Northwest Museum of Arts and Culture.

media.alt: A parade scene on a city street with a crowd of people gathered on the left, including children and adults. Banners and flags are strung overhead. A person on stilts is visible in the middle, and a marching band with instruments like saxophones and trumpets can be seen coming down the street. Buildings line both sides of the street, with some signs visible, including "TED Cigar Stores Company." A "STOP" sign is hanging high above the street.
media.caption: Spokane Pow Wow Parade

text.headline: Spokane Pow Wow Parade
text.text: Parade in Spokane Pow Wow event featuring a performer in a powdered wig and stilts with marching band playing on the street behind him.

media.alt: A detailed illustration features a large, ornate building with multiple columns and a pointed tower. Several cars and small figures are depicted on the street in front of the building. A map on the right shows the region labeled as "Inland Empire" with Spokane highlighted. Lines resembling rays extend from Spokane to Seattle, Tacoma, and Portland, marked with distances. Text at the top reads "The Spokesman-Review and Spokane Daily Chronicle," and "Cover Spokane and Inland Empire Like the Sunshine." Additional text at the bottom includes "Enlarged Home of the Cowles Publications," and "Heart of Pacific Northwest 'The American Wonderland,'" with "Spokane Floor 621 Mallon."
media.caption: Spokesman-Review Letterhead

text.headline: Spokesman-Review Letterhead
text.text: Detail from a correspondence between the paper and Spokane Flour Mills.

media.alt: A map labeled "Cape Colony" with surrounding areas such as "Orange Free State" and "Basuto Land." The map provides details like "Area, 219,700 Sq. M" and "Pop., 1,252,347 Gov., British Colony." To the left, there is an illustration depicting an oil palm tree, a mining scene labeled "Diamond Fields," and a coastal city with Table Mountain in the background. Text at the bottom reads "View of Cape Town."
media.caption: Illustration of South African Dutch Colonial Interests

text.headline: Illustration of South African Dutch Colonial Interests
text.text: Overlapping illustrations of scenes in South Africa, including a ship's perspective view of Cape Town and a bird's eye perspective of diamond mine operations.

media.alt: A large open-pit mining site with steep rock walls and various mining structures on the edge. Equipment and cables are visible on the site. The text at the bottom reads: "Kimberley. Mines de diamant de Beers."
media.caption: Kimberely, Mines de Diamant de Beers

text.headline: Kimberely, Mines de Diamant de Beers
text.text: Photograph of diamond operations in the Kimberly Mines, controlled by the British De Beers company.

media.alt: Two men working inside a mine. One man is sitting and appears to be examining something in his hands, while the other stands and holds a tool. The surrounding walls are rocky. The text at the bottom reads, "Travailleurs dans les mines de diamant."
media.caption: Travailleurs days les Mines de Diamant

text.headline: Travailleurs days les Mines de Diamant
text.text: Photograph of two laborers working inside the Kimberely Mines.

media.alt: Various pieces of ornate jewelry are displayed on a red background. These include brooches, rings, and decorative pins. Intricate designs feature pearls, gold, and gemstones. The top of the image has the text "DIE PERLE" and at the bottom "Konvolut s.ges v. Morin Gerlach" and "Sammtlich deponirt."
media.caption: Die Perle, Chromolithograph Illustration

text.headline: Die Perle, Chromolithograph Illustration
text.text: Illustration of a series of gold, diamond and pearl jewelry.

media.alt: A bearded man with a receding hairline and wearing a suit is seen in profile. His attire includes a vest, a jacket, and a tie. The background is plain.
media.caption: Portrait of James J. Hill

text.headline: Portrait of James J. Hill
text.text: Studio portrait of the founder of the Great Northern Railroad company.

media.alt: Map depicting the Great Northern Railway and its connections across the northwestern United States and parts of Canada. The map shows railway lines traversing through states like Minnesota, North Dakota, Montana, Washington, and Oregon, as well as parts of British Columbia and Alberta. A red circle is marked on the map. Text on the map includes "Great Northern Railway and Connections" and promotional information about opportunities for homes and employment along the railway.
media.caption: Great Northern Railway and Connections

text.headline: Great Northern Railway and Connections
text.text: Map of the Great Nothern Railway stations, reaching from St. Paul, MN to Seattle, WA.

media.alt: The image features a Dutch text titled "Naar Chinook! Naar Montana!" which translates to "To Chinook! To Montana!" It discusses the agricultural prospects in Montana, highlighting statements from Professor Shaw of the State Agricultural School in St. Paul, who recognizes Montana as a leading agricultural state. The text mentions Montana's achievements at the National Dry Farming Congress and emphasizes successful dry farming even during drought years. It encourages Dutch farmers to see the opportunities in Montana for themselves, noting the potential for high wheat yields around Chinook. The text aims to attract farmers to move to Montana, describing the region's benefits and available homesteads. Additionally, it provides travel details for those interested.
media.caption: Advertisement for Dutch Settlement in Montana

text.headline: Advertisement for Dutch Settlement in Montana
text.text: Advertisement for the Chinook, Montana settlement in a Wisconsin-based Dutch language newspaper.

media.alt: A man with a mustache and beard is wearing a double-breasted coat and a high-collared shirt, looking to the side.
media.caption: Herman Antonie Van Valkenburg

text.headline: Herman Antonie Van Valkenburg
text.text: Studio portrait

media.alt: An illustration of a scenic landscape featuring a tall tree in the foreground, a rocky mountainside, and a body of water. Behind the water is a range of hills and a sun setting. The text reads "SPOKANE AND THE INLAND EMPIRE." There is a circular logo with the words "NORTHERN PACIFIC."
media.caption: Spokane and the Inland Empire

text.headline: Spokane and the Inland Empire
text.text: Booster pamphlet cover illustration

media.alt: A detailed cityscape illustration with buildings, roads, and houses spread across a hilly landscape. The foreground features a railway line with a streetcar and scattered houses surrounded by trees. The background shows a densely built urban area. The text at the bottom reads: "Spokane Falls—View of Cook’s Motor Line from Spokane Heights, Elevation 350 Feet." The signature "Winsor" is visible.
media.caption: View of Cook's Motor Line from Spokane Heights

text.headline: View of Cook's Motor Line from Spokane Heights
text.text: Bird's eye Spokane residential area illustration

media.alt: Text reading "Phoenix Lumber Company" is over an illustration of trees. Below the main text, it says "Spokane, Washington."
media.caption: Phoenix Lumber Company Letterhead

text.headline: Phoenix Lumber Company Letterhead
text.text: Detail from correspondence between the company and the Arcadia Valley Fruit Grower's Association

media.alt: A group of eleven men sit and stand closely together in a room, holding various musical instruments such as trombones, violins, a clarinet, a trumpet, and a drum. Most are dressed in suits with bow ties or ties. A curtain and wall decorations are visible in the background.
media.caption: Travelling Musicians in Spokane

text.headline: Travelling Musicians in Spokane
text.text: Travelling traditional Dutch musicians photographed in a Spokane residence.

media.alt: A view of a river with strong rapids, flowing past a rocky landscape. On the left, there are several industrial buildings, including a large brick structure with multiple windows. To the right, stacks of lumber are piled on a rocky hill, with a sign nearby that reads "E. A. WILL & CO SHINGLES WHITE PINE PRICES 1.25X." In the background, a metal truss bridge spans the river, with additional buildings and signs visible in the distance.
media.caption: Spokane Falls, Below Power Station

text.headline: Spokane Falls, Below Power Station
text.text: Phoenix Saw Mill on right, Spokane Flour Mills on left.

media.alt: A collage of architectural images including several buildings and a bridge. Text at the top reads, "Souvenir Card, Spokane, Washington, 1899." The images show structures labeled as "Granite Block," "City Hall," "Oriental Building," "Monroe St. Bridge," and "Bancroft School." The images are surrounded by decorative patterns.
media.caption: Souvenir Card, Spokane, Washington, 1899

text.headline: Souvenir Card, Spokane, Washington, 1899
text.text: Collage of four buildings and the Monroe St. Bridge.

media.alt: An ornate interior with tall green marble columns, decorative ceiling details, and multiple hanging light fixtures. There is a central wooden table in the middle of the space. The surrounding area has teller windows with dark frames. The text reads: "Interior Old National Bank, Spokane, Wash."
media.caption: Interior Old National Bank

text.headline: Interior Old National Bank
text.text: Postcard of the Old National Bank interior, featuring two rows of green marble columns.

media.alt: A man with a mustache and a short beard is shown wearing a suit jacket, a white shirt, and a dark tie. He has short, neatly combed hair and is looking slightly to the side.
media.caption: Thomas G. Thomson

text.headline: Thomas G. Thomson
text.text: Studio portrait

media.alt: The image presents a title page with text in Dutch. The main title reads "Nederlanders in Amerika," followed by a subtitle about emigrants and colonies in the United States during the 19th and 20th centuries. It mentions that this is an academic dissertation for a doctoral degree in literature and philosophy at the University of Amsterdam, under the supervision of Dr. H. Burger. The author's name is Jacob van Hinte. There are two stamps: one from the "Philologisch Historisch Instituut" and another from the "Universitatis Groninganae Bibliotheca." The publisher is P. Noordhoff in Groningen, dated 1928.
media.caption: Nederlanders in Amerika

text.headline: Nederlanders in Amerika
text.text: Copyright Page of Nederlanders in Amerika

media.alt: A typed letter on letterhead from Northwestern and Pacific Hypotheekbank, dated March 26th, 1898, in Spokane, Washington. It is addressed to Alfred R. Stratton discussing the sale of property and insurance. The letter mentions a $700 insurance policy for a building from June 10th, 1897, to June 10th, 1900, with a premium of $10.50. The recipient is offered the insurance at a proportional rate of $7.90. It is signed by Thomas G. Thomson, Manager.
media.caption: Northwestern and Pacific Hypotheekbank Correspondence

text.headline: Northwestern and Pacific Hypotheekbank Correspondence
text.text: Correspondence between Thomson and Alfred R. Stratton

media.alt: A historical mural depicting a scene from the era of the Monroe Doctrine in 1823. Several men in 19th-century attire are gathered around a table with one man pointing at a globe. To the left, a military figure stands with a sword and another person stands beneath a palm tree. To the right, two men in traditional attire carry rifles. Below the mural reads, "THE MONROE DOCTRINE - 1823." Beneath the scene, text states: "WE MUST REMEMBER THAT ANY OPPRESSION ANY INJUSTICE ANY HATRED IS A WEDGE DESIGNED TO ATTACK OUR CIVILIZATION. FRANKLIN DELANO ROOSEVELT 1940." Decorative elements and classical motifs frame the images and text.
media.caption: The Monroe Doctrine, 1823

text.headline: The Monroe Doctrine, 1823
text.text: Oil on canvas, Great Experiment Hall, Cox Corridors, Us. Capitol.

media.alt: A man with a long beard is holding two large magnets labeled "United States" and "Protection" over a body of water. Small figures labeled "Cuba," "Puerto Rico," "Hawaii," and "Philippines" are being drawn toward the magnets. A tree with a bent trunk is labeled "Manhattan." An explosion is visible in the background. The text at the bottom reads "The Pull of the Monroe Magnet."
media.caption: The Pull of the Monroe Magnet

text.headline: The Pull of the Monroe Magnet
text.text: Political cartoon featured in Puck Magazine.

media.alt: A man in a coat and striped pants, labeled Uncle Sam, stands at the entrance of a large gate labeled "United States." The gate has a sign saying "PROTECTION." Behind the gate, several people carrying bags appear to be entering. Above the entrance is a shield with "U.S." and the words "Public Schools." In the background are sailing ships. The text reads: "Uncle Sam sees the danger. Uncle Sam—These doors must be shut at once. I have no more room for such as these. Self-protection is the first law of Nature." The initials L.A.D. are at the bottom right.
media.caption: Uncle Sam Sees the Danger

text.headline: Uncle Sam Sees the Danger
text.text: Political cartoon

media.alt: A person wearing a double-breasted coat and a hat adorned with feathers. The text below reads "Pat Kanim - Puget Sound Indian."
media.caption: Pat Kanim, Snoqualmie Chief

text.headline: Pat Kanim, Snoqualmie Chief
text.text: Photographic print on cabinet card (copied from an earlier tintype, possibly 1850s), ca. 1855 (printed after 1870).

media.alt: A large industrial site with a building labeled "The Sawmill Phoenix Lumber." Stacks of lumber are arranged throughout the area. Wooden ramps connect the building to other parts of the yard. Horses and wagons are visible, some marked with "Phoenix Lumber Company" and the address "Wholesale, Lumber, Coal, Wood." Railway tracks run along one side, and there are several buildings and smokestacks in the background.
media.caption: The "Sawmill Phoenix"

text.headline: The "Sawmill Phoenix"
text.text: Phoenix Lumber Company and Sawmill on the riverbank at the foot of Post and Wall Streets.

media.alt: Spokane, Washington promotional card featuring a bridge over water with text reading "City of Sunshine and Power," "Matchless opportunities for manufacturer and farmer," "Three million horsepower available, 145 thousand horsepower developed," and "Center of vast empire rich in forests, grains, fruits, mines. Supports 800,000 people." The phrase "Where power is cheap" is at the bottom.
media.caption: City of Sunshine and Power Booster Pamphlet

text.headline: City of Sunshine and Power Booster Pamphlet
text.text: Brochure published by the Spokane company as a celebration of the 18th anniversary of their business with cover illustration engraving work done by the American Engraving Company of Spokane.

media.alt: An illustration of a river with waterfalls and industrial buildings along the banks. A bridge spans across the river in the background. Text on the image reads "Main Power of the Spokane River, The Property of the Washington Water Power Co." and "Washington Water Power Co.'s Upper Power."
media.caption: Main Power of the Spokane River

text.headline: Main Power of the Spokane River
text.text: Illustration of the Spokane Falls and surrounding businesses.

media.alt: A bearded man in a suit with a bow tie, shown in an oval frame, looking to the left.
media.caption: E. F. Cartier Van Dissel

text.headline: E. F. Cartier Van Dissel
text.text: Studio portrait

media.alt: A newspaper page with the headline "Realty -- Doings of the Spokane Dealers -- Business." Subheadline reads "Cut-Over Lands Near Springdale Are Being Sold to Settlers." Three images show lands with cut trees and a small building. The text discusses cut-over timber lands near Springdale in Stevens County, Washington, sold by the Phoenix Lumber Company. Immigrants are settling in these areas, with hundreds of acres being developed. The text highlights the potential for diversified farming, dairying, and stock raising, and notes that activities will boost the local economy.
media.caption: Article on Dutch Settlement in Springdale

text.headline: Article on Dutch Settlement in Springdale
text.text: Advertisement for the Springdale, WA settlement in English-language newspaper.

media.alt: An open book with handwritten text and illustrations. The left page reads "Chineesche Spiritisme" and "Blijspel in 3 bedrijven door Ning-Po," featuring artwork of a small red figure and a decorative letter "C." The right page contains handwritten text under the heading "Personen" with decorative illustrations, including various small figures and a man in traditional attire.
media.caption: Diary Entry

text.headline: Diary Entry
text.text: A short story written by the author titled "Chinese Spiritualism", featuring a series of racialize illustrations of Chinese caricatures, dragons and devils.

media.alt: A large building with a red roof and arches, featuring multiple stories and windows. The text reads "3889. SPOKANE ATHLETIC CLUB, SPOKANE, WASH." An American flag is visible hanging from one of the windows. A partial postmark is seen on the right, suggesting it's a mailed card.
media.caption: Spokane Athletic Club

text.headline: Spokane Athletic Club
text.text: Postcard of the Spokane Athletic Club.

media.alt: A flyer with a decorative border featuring a Star of David symbol at the top. The text reads: "First Annual Minstrel Show" presented by the "Spokane Amateur Athletic Club." The event is scheduled for "February 11th, '13" at "Auditorium Theatre." A small illustration of a character in the center. Additional text describes the entertainment as a mix of songs and comedy, with references to "colored comedy" and "old time Negro melodies." The bottom credits "Keystone Printing Co., 110 South Lincoln St."
media.caption: First Annual Minstrel Show

text.headline: First Annual Minstrel Show
text.text: Brochure for event at the Spokane Auditorium Theater

media.alt: A woman in a white and pink dress is in a kitchen, stirring a barrel with a spoon. A young boy peeks from behind the barrel. On the left, a dog sits near a broken jar labeled "Centennial Mills Roller Mills Gold Drop Flour." A chair, a cabinet with a door ajar, and a shelf with a candle and a bowl are visible. A box labeled "Wheat Manna for Breakfast" is on the floor. The text "Manufactured by Centennial Mill Co. Spokane, Wash., U.S.A." is at the bottom. The words "Injured Innocence" are also written on the floor.
media.caption: Injured Innocence

text.headline: Injured Innocence

media.alt: A box labeled "Wheat Manna for Breakfast" from Centennial Mill Company, Spokane, Wash. features a circular illustration showing a person and a child. The side of the box reads "Made Out Of Pacific Coast White Wheat."

text.headline: Injured Innocence, Detail
text.text: Detail of trade card advertisement for Centennial Mills Company.

media.alt: An illustrated map of Spokane Falls highlighting the town's layout with streets and rivers. The map includes banners pointing to various locations and resources such as "Granite Sandstone Slate & Asbestos," "The Big Bend & Stock Country," and "Tobacco Plantations." The map also mentions nearby mining areas, farming districts, and industrial sites. Text at the top left corner reads "SPOKANE FALLS and her natural resources, the variety and extent of which are not equalled by any city in the world."
media.caption: Spokane Falls and Her Natural Resources

text.headline: Spokane Falls and Her Natural Resources
text.text: Map of the natural resources in and around Spokane for the 1890 Northwest Industrial Exposition.

media.alt: A map-like illustration showing a grid of streets and buildings. It includes labeled areas such as "Stock Ranges," "Tobacco Plantations," and "To the Palouse Farming & Fruit Districts. Finest in the World." A pointing hand is drawn next to each label. A railway is depicted leading "to the coast."
media.caption: Spokane Falls and Her Natural Resources, Detail

text.headline: Spokane Falls and Her Natural Resources, Detail
text.text: Detail of a map of the natural resources in and around Spokane for the 1890 Northwest Industrial Exposition.

media.alt: A fabric banner with fringe at the bottom. Text on the banner reads: "I.O.G.T." followed by an image of a globe with the words "Our Field" across it. Below the globe, it says "Northern Light" and "Lodge No. 70." The location given is "Spokane Falls, Washington."
media.caption: Northern Light, Lodge No. 70

text.headline: Northern Light, Lodge No. 70
text.text: Ribbon from the organization.

media.alt: A large group of people gathered outside a two-story building with columns and ornate architecture. Many individuals are in lines, while others form a crowd on the sidewalk. Trees and additional buildings are visible in the background. Several people are riding bicycles or standing with them, and some are dressed in hats and formal clothing.
media.caption: Spokane -- Parades (#07)

text.headline: Spokane -- Parades (#07)
text.text: Procession of Masonic Blue Lodges to new Temple on Riverside Avenue at dedication.

media.alt: A group of armored figures standing at attention hold spears near a set of wide steps. Another figure in a robe holds scales while seated at the top. Near them, several individuals are engaged in a chase or struggle. Above, a banner with "F.C.B." is displayed.
media.caption: Damon and Pythias Illustration

text.headline: Damon and Pythias Illustration
text.text: Depiction of the characters Damon and Pythias beneath a banner with the letters FCB, standing for friendship, charity and benevolence.

media.alt: A certificate of identification labeled "FR-33015" from The Fraternal Identification Co. of America, Inc. Text describes procedures in case of accident or sickness, indicating that information will be sent to relatives. It states the company will provide assistance but not cover expenses without an authorized order. The document is signed by "Chas H. White" as President and is marked void after "8/21/07." Next to the text is a circular image showing a man wearing a hat and glasses.
media.caption: Charles Woodruff Clarke

text.headline: Charles Woodruff Clarke
text.text: Fraternal Identification Card.

media.alt: Shriner Cecil Walwrath in brown face makeup and pseudo Middle Eastern dress.

text.headline: Shriner Cecil Walwrath
text.text: Kodachrome stereoscopic images of Shriner Cecil Walwrath in brown face makeup and pseudo-Middle Eastern dress.

media.alt:
A depiction of two factory buildings is presented. The top image shows a large multi-story factory labeled as "Office and Main Factory," surrounded by trees and a few smaller buildings. Smoke is emanating from a chimney. The lower image shows another expansive factory labeled "Factory No. 2 Lodge Room Furniture and Lodge Properties," with several buildings and a train nearby. 

Text at the top reads: "SIXTEEN FACTORIES IN ONE OVER THREE ACRES FLOOR SPACE." A list of departments includes Costumes, Regalia, Uniforms, Helmets, Caps, Leather Shop, Metal Shop, and more. 

A message to the left of the lower building image says: "You; Can Depend On Quality First and Service Always. These Features Will Mean Much To All Our Patrons."

At the bottom, the text reads: "THE HENDERSON-AMES CO. KALAMAZOO, MICHIGAN."
media.caption: Sixteen Factories in One

text.headline: Sixteen Factories in One
text.text: Back cover of the company's catalogue with illustrations of the facilities from various angles.

media.alt: An illustration of a woman in profile is surrounded by elaborate floral and decorative borders. The text reads: "Importers of fine Parisian novelties, Tar and Egg Julep Shampoo. Miller & Dervant French Hair Dressers, Costumers and Wig Makers. Spokane, Wash., 509 Sprague Ave. 124 Post St. between Riverside & Main." The telephone number "Tel 642" is also included.
media.caption: Miller-Dervant Business Card

text.headline: Miller-Dervant Business Card
text.text: Ornate engraved business card for the Spokane-based costumers and wig designers.

media.alt: Five illustrated figures depicting costume ideas. From left to right, top to bottom: a figure labeled "Romeo" in a classic Renaissance outfit, a figure labeled "Page" dressed in medieval attire, a figure labeled "Negro Minstrel" in minstrel show clothing, a figure labeled "Uncle Sam" in a patriotic costume with a top hat and striped pants, a figure labeled "Polar Bear" in a bear costume, and a figure labeled "American Dude" in formal attire with a top hat and cane. Text at the top reads "Gentlemen’s Costumes—Continued" with an address "209-211 N. Post St., Spokane, Washington, U.S.A." at the very top. At the bottom, there is text about costume and character make-up for events, along with pricing details.
media.caption: Gentleman's Costumes

text.headline: Gentleman's Costumes
text.text: Sample of costumes available, including Uncle Sam, Negro Minstrel and Polar Bear.

media.alt: Illustration of various people in theatrical costumes forming a circular arrangement. Some figures are sitting, dancing, or interacting with each other. Inside the circle, text reads: "We contract to 'make-up' and costume plays: operas, dramas, comedies, sketches and minstrels. Let us have a copy of your score or play-book, with approximate sizes. It will save time. Correspondence solicited. We ship costumes anywhere."
media.caption: We Contract to Makeup and Costume Plays

text.headline: We Contract to Makeup and Costume Plays
text.text: Back cover illustration of the 1906 Miller-Dervant costume catalogue.

media.alt: A large group of people are gathered in an open area, dressed in uniform outfits, possibly participating in a coordinated event or parade. In the background, there's a hillside with scattered buildings and a large bridge with arches spanning a river. A crowd of spectators is sitting, some holding umbrellas, watching the event. Flags are visible on the bridge.
media.caption: The Dance of the Dutch

text.headline: The Dance of the Dutch
text.text: Photo that may be documenting an event from the 1913 Spokane Pow Wow, taken from the bleachers of Glover Field, then known as The Stadium, looking out onto the Monroe Bridge and Spokane River.

media.alt: A group of young children dressed in traditional clothing are standing in rows on an open field, each holding a stick above their heads. Some children are wearing pointed hats and their outfits have striped patterns. They appear to be participating in a coordinated activity or performance.
media.caption: The Dance of the Dutch, Detail

text.headline: The Dance of the Dutch, Detail
text.text: Detail of photograph that may be documenting an event from the 1913 Spokane Pow Wow.

media.alt: Group of people next to a wooden cabinet with decorative plates. Each holds a musical instrument, including an accordion, trumpet, and guitar. Text reads, "Songs as Sung at the Dutch Mill of the Coeur d'Alene Hotel in Spokane. Staff of waiters who entertain and serve you." A note at the bottom says, "Where cares are forgotten, fellowship abounds, and life and happiness are one. A Souvenir Copy of this Song Folder will be mailed to any address for 10c."
media.caption: Songs as Sung at the Dutch Mill

text.headline: Songs as Sung at the Dutch Mill
text.text: Cover of the song folder for the Dutch Mill.

media.alt: Banner for Holland Advertising Co., featuring the text: "Holland Advertising Co. Programme Advertising. General Commercial Artists Show-Cards Price-Tickets & Posters 717 Main Avenue. Telephone 2004." The design includes a central illustration of a person in a windmill, surrounded by decorative elements and the word "Signs" written vertically on both sides.
media.caption: Business Card

text.headline: Business Card
text.text: Business card featuring an illustration of a young woman in pigtails in front of a windmill.

media.alt:
A drawing of a man with wings, dressed in a long coat and hat, standing on a pedestal. He holds a corn cob above his head, emitting lines reminiscent of rays. Below, text reads:

"und puy dot leetle poy von  
FLYER FLEXIBLE  
to goast mit de hill down, und see his vather  
schmile oud loud ven he see dot poy steer mit  
his hans, und not vear dose poots oud some  
more. And auf you vant a schled mit horses  
on, dey got some ghoot vones. Yah!"
media.caption: Advertisement

text.headline: Advertisement
text.text: Advertisement featuring a caricature of a German-American.

media.alt: A large ship named "Great Northern" is sailing in the water, with flags strung along its mast. A smaller boat is visible in the background on the left. The scene depicts a calm maritime setting.
media.caption: SS Great Northern

text.headline: SS Great Northern
text.text: Great Northern Railway steamship on the Columbia River near Flavel, Oregon.

media.alt: A storefront with a sign reading "Oriental Baths and Laundry" and "Laundry Baths 15¢." The address "210" is above the entrance to one side. There are towels displayed in the window. On the side, there is an oval portrait of a man in a suit. Text on the right side advertises "T.S. Billiard Cigars & Tob. 208" and "OBAK Mouthpiece Cigarettes." Japanese characters are present on the right and left borders.
media.caption: Oriental Baths and Laundry

text.headline: Oriental Baths and Laundry
text.text: Photograph of Spokane storefront.

media.alt: A street scene with a parade featuring people carrying banners and lanterns decorated with flowers. A child holding an umbrella walks in the middle of the street. Both sides of the street are lined with spectators, many wearing hats. Buildings in the background have signs reading "Boston" and "Peerless," and American flags are visible.
media.caption: Spokane Parade Scene

text.headline: Spokane Parade Scene
text.text:
"Chinese themed parade participants on
Riverside Avenue"

media.alt: A fabric sack with the text "Tai Chi Company Foochow" and an image of a horse inside a circular frame. Below the horse, it reads "Manufactured by Spokane Flour Mills Spokane, U.S.A." The text at the bottom says "Bleached Flour Net Weight 49 Lbs. When Packed Horse Brand."
media.caption: Tai Chi Company Flour Sack

text.headline: Tai Chi Company Flour Sack
text.text: Spokane Flour Mills wholesale flour design for trade in Chinese markets.

media.alt: A cloth flour sack labeled "'PATO' Brand" features an illustration of a duck with the words "Uniformity" and "Purity" written on ribbons. The text includes "Finest Bluestem Blend Flour," "49 lbs. net," "Bleached," and "Milled by Spokane Flour Mills, Spokane, Wash. U.S.A." It mentions "Genato Commercial Corporation" as the exclusive representative in Manila, Philippine Islands. The phrase "'PATO' Flour" is repeated on the sides.
media.caption: Pato Brand Flour Sack

text.headline: Pato Brand Flour Sack
text.text: Spokane Flour Mills wholesale flour design for trade in Filipino markets.

media.alt: A fabric sack with text and illustrations. The text reads "Orchard Brand" prominently at the top, followed by "Bleached Flour U.S.A.," and "Tai Chi Company." At the bottom, it states "49 Lbs. Net When Packed Orchard Brand." There are Chinese characters on it as well. The illustration features flowers and leaves near a rock.
media.caption: Orchard Brand Flour Sack

text.headline: Orchard Brand Flour Sack
text.text: Spokane Flour Mills wholesale flour design for trade in Chinese markets.

media.alt: A parade on a busy street with five people riding horses. Four are in uniform, and one is wearing a headdress. Crowds line both sides of the street, with flags and banners hanging from surrounding buildings. Signs in the background include "Carr's," "Casino," and "Coca-Cola."
media.caption: Raymond E. Bigelow Leading the Pow Wow

text.headline: Raymond E. Bigelow Leading the Pow Wow
text.text: Director of the Spokane Ad Club Raymond E. Bigelow leading the Pow Wow Celebration in brown face make up and Indigenous dress as "Big Chief I".

media.alt: A woman dressed in a decorated long dress stands holding hands with a young girl. The woman wears a headband labeled "Spokane" and her dress features a sun design and patterned belt. The girl is in a dress with a sash labeled "Portland" and holds a rose. Text at the bottom reads "Compliments of THE SPOKANE AD CLUB Copyright 1913."
media.caption: Miss Spokane and Queen Caroline Testout

text.headline: Miss Spokane and Queen Caroline Testout
text.text: Marguerite Motie as Miss Spokane and four year-old Mildred Tucker as Queen Caroline Testout.

media.alt: A woman in a traditional dress stands pointing to a map. Text reads "Miss Spokane Invites You to Spokane in the heart of the Pacific Northwest" and "The American Wonderland." The map includes outlines of Washington, Oregon, Idaho, Montana, and Wyoming, with rail lines and a heart symbol on Spokane.
media.caption: The American Wonderland

text.headline: The American Wonderland
text.text: Illustration of Marguerite Motie as Miss Spokane above a map of the Pacific Northwest.

media.alt: Big Chief I Bids You Be Merry is written at the top. The central image is of a man wearing a traditional Native American-style headdress and regalia, labeled "Big Chief 1." To the left, there is an oval image of a woman identified as "Miss Spokane," wearing a headband with "Spokane" written on it. Below her is a text block describing the Spokane Ad Club, Raymond E. Bigelow, and various civic contributions. To the right is an oval image of a man identified as "President F. M. March of Pow Wow." Decorative elements and small illustrations of a Native American chief in profile are present.
media.caption: Big Chief I Bids You Be Merry

text.headline: Big Chief I Bids You Be Merry
text.text: Introduction to the program, featuring Raymond E. Bigelow, Marguerite Motie as Miss Spokane and Spokane irrigation leader Fm. March.

media.alt: A street scene with a parade featuring covered wagons and people on horseback moving along a city thoroughfare. Onlookers, some wearing hats, are gathered on the sidewalk watching the procession. Nearby buildings have awnings and signage, including "Whites Shoe Shop," "Hub Clothing," "Michigan Hotel," "Auto Loan," and "Drugs." There are several automobiles parked along the street. A man in uniform is standing in the street near the crowd.
media.caption: Frontier Recreation Scene in Parade

text.headline: Frontier Recreation Scene in Parade
text.text: Parade showing wagons, streetcars and buses.

media.alt: Lunette decorations featuring artistic figures in the Old National Bank, Spokane. Below, an interior view of the banking room displays an ornate dome ceiling, walls with decorative arches, and a central statue. The architect is L. L. Rand. The text indicates the source as The Western Architect, September 1908.
media.caption: Lunette Decorations in the Old National Bank

text.headline: Lunette Decorations in the Old National Bank
text.text: Lunette decorations and interior view of bank building, designed by architect All. Rand.

media.alt: A decorative arch features a scene with a person sitting and engaging with a child who is sitting on the ground. Behind them is a bundle of wheat. The arch is adorned with ornamental patterns and the text "GLORIA IN EXCELSIS DEO" along the curve.
media.caption: Lunette Decorations in the Old National Bank, Detail

text.headline: Lunette Decorations in the Old National Bank, Detail
text.text: Lunette decorations and interior view of bank building, designed by architect All. Rand.

media.alt: A decorative page featuring text reading "Illustrated Annual" and "The Spokane Spokesman." An illustration shows a woman sitting with a draped cloth, holding a paper with "New Year Greetings." There is an image of a cityscape, medallions labeled "Seal of the State of Washington" and "Seal of the State of Idaho," and a label saying "January 1st, Spokane, Washington, 1892." The page includes ornate designs and illustrations.
media.caption: New Year's Greetings

text.headline: New Year's Greetings
text.text: Cover illustration for the Annual Illustrated Spokane Spokesman.

media.alt: A woman in a white dress holds a guitar while seated between an ornate pedestal and a decorative stone. She has flowers pinned to her dress. The background features a blurred natural scene. Text at the bottom reads "Sam'l D. Owings, 816 Riverside Ave., Spokane, Wash."
media.caption: Studio Portrait with Classical Iconography

text.headline: Studio Portrait with Classical Iconography
text.text: Example of studio portraiture in Spokane with unknown model.

media.alt: A person wearing a long white garment standing on what appears to be straw or dry grass. Behind them is a stone structure with ornate carvings, including a face and decorative patterns.
media.caption: Studio Portrait with Classical Iconography, Detail

text.headline: Studio Portrait with Classical Iconography, Detail
text.text: Detail of studio portraiture in Spokane highlighting horsehair mats imitating grass within a controlled studio space.

media.alt: A group of women dressed in elaborate period costumes are parading down a street. They wear long, elegant dresses with large, decorative hats or wigs. Onlookers line the street, and buildings in the background are decorated with flags and banners. A sign in the background reads "RAINIER GRAND HOTEL."
media.caption: Marie Antoinettes

text.headline: Marie Antoinettes
text.text: Scattered group of parade participants wearing French Noblewomen costumes on Riverside Avenue.

media.alt: An ornate room features a large, intricately carved fireplace with two decorative columns on either side. To the right, there is a curved bookshelf filled with books. A round table with a small decorative item and an open book is positioned at the center of the room, surrounded by elegant chairs. In the background, a potted plant and table with a lamp are visible, along with a curved sofa and additional chairs.
media.caption: Library with Classical Iconography

text.headline: Library with Classical Iconography
text.text: Interior of residential library in an issue of the Inland Empire Architect magazine.

media.alt: A room with green vertical striped walls and matching curtains. The ceiling is decorated with floral patterns and garlands. There is a white fireplace with ornate details, housing a fire. On the mantelpiece are two red vases and a small object. A framed picture hangs on the wall above the fireplace. The text "Library or Sitting Room" is at the bottom.
media.caption: Green Velvet Effect Wallpaper

text.headline: Green Velvet Effect Wallpaper
text.text: Illustration from Wall Papers; Artistic Wall Hangings, nod.

media.alt: Two men in a room with painted wall decorations showing horses and a carriage. One man is wearing a hat and holding a brush, while the other stands near a table. The room contains a ladder and various paint supplies.
media.caption: Circus Room Mural

text.headline: Circus Room Mural
text.text: Louis Davenport and Abraham Rutgers Van der Loeff in the "Circus Room".

media.alt: On the left, a person is seen standing beside what appears to be a horse. On the right, a person is shown looking upward, dressed in a shirt with rolled-up sleeves, with a circular object above them.
media.caption: Sign of the Calling

text.headline: Sign of the Calling
text.text: Detail of the Circus Room Mural by Abraham Rutgers Van der Loeff depicting a man presenting a Masonic hand gesture behind his back and a young man nearby mimicking the sign.

media.alt: A promotional illustration for "Valley-View Addition to Spokane" featuring a plotted map with numbered tracts. The headline encourages owning a home to avoid paying rent and emphasizes good schools, neighborhood amenities, and irrigation purposes. There's an image of a substation and depot under construction. Details about pricing and terms are included, highlighting equal tract sizes and water rights. The text promotes easy access via the new Coeur d'Alene Electric Railway, 30 minutes from Spokane. Maps show proximity to the Spokane River and nearby lakes.
media.caption: Valley View Addition

text.headline: Valley View Addition
text.text: Promotional booster map for an irrigated addition of logged-over farmland outside of Spokane.

media.alt: A group of men wearing suits and ties are seated on either side of a long dining table, set with plates, cutlery, and floral arrangements in the center. Two women stand in the background by a window. A sign in the lower right corner reads "C. M. Hayes Pittsburgh, PA."
media.caption: Spokane Commission Dinner

text.headline: Spokane Commission Dinner
text.text: Dinner held in the Education Building during the Alaska Yukon Pacific Exposition in Seattle. E.F. Cartier Van Dissel seated in the foreground at the right.

media.alt: Document for the "National Apple Show" held in Spokane, Washington. The exhibition dates are December 7 to 12, 1908, with $25,000 in prizes. The document lists officials including L. W. Hill as President and L. F. Williams as First Vice President. It also includes the show's capital stock amount and features two red apples. Offices are located at 223-224 Hutton Building, Spokane, Wash, USA. The date November 24, 1908, is written at the bottom.
media.caption: Exposition of Apples

text.headline: Exposition of Apples
text.text: Letterhead detail from the National Apple Show, noting Elf. Cartier Van Dissel (Phoenix Sawmill and Lumber Company), Henry M. Richards (Washington Water Power Company) and Lois W. Hill (Great Northern Railway) as associates.

media.alt: A busy scene with numerous cartoon characters engaging in various activities outside a building labeled "The Fourth Nat'l Apple Show." Some characters hold apples and signs. Speech bubbles include phrases like "Well here we are again, by heck," "His majesty King Pip VI," and "Have you seen the Monroe Street Bridge?" Flags decorate the background. Text at the top reads "Doings in Apple Land No. 4" and at the bottom, "The arrival in Spokaue."
media.caption: The Arrival in Spokane

text.headline: The Arrival in Spokane
text.text: Cartoon depicting the arrival of the apple caricatures in Spokane in front of the Old Armory Building.

media.alt: Notice of termination of tenancy addressed to B. M. Miller and Beulah Miller, doing business as Miller-Dervant. It requires them to quit the premises located at No. 209-211 Post Street, Spokane, Washington, by December 31, 1933, with the intention to terminate the tenancy. The document is dated in Spokane, Washington, on the fifth day of December 1933. It includes text from Arthur D. Jones & Co., Real Estate & Insurance, and is signed by an owner named Thomas.
media.caption: Termination of Tenancy

text.headline: Termination of Tenancy
text.text: Notice issued from the bank to the Miller-Dervant costume company.

media.alt: A float in a parade pulled by horses. It is decorated with flags and features wooden panels. A banner on the side reads "Phoenix Lumber Co. J. Bendickson, Mgr. Lake Mary." Trees and a person walking are in the background.
media.caption: Phoenix Lumber Company Float

text.headline: Phoenix Lumber Company Float
text.text: Horse-drawn parade float on a dirt road.

media.alt: An illustration of a windmill with visible blades sits above a detailed drawing of a building and trees. Across the center, there is bold text that reads "A. R. van der Loeff."
media.caption: A. R. Van der Loeff Show Cards

text.headline: A. R. Van der Loeff Show Cards
text.text: Letterhead illustration for the commercial artist from an invoice directed to Spokane Flour Mills.

media.alt:
A collection of four artistic illustrations, each with its own theme:

1. A view of Cape Town with a map labeled "Cape Colony," featuring oil palm and diamond fields, and a coastal scene with sailboats.
2. A map labeled "British Guiana" with images of a pepper plantation and individuals involved in pepper harvesting, alongside a portrait of a person.
3. A map labeled "Argentine Republic" accompanied by scenes of a rider throwing a bola and a cityscape of Buenos Aires with sailing boats.
4. An illustration themed around Siam (Thailand), featuring an elephant with handlers, a map labeled "Siam," and a scene of boats in the "Floating City of Bangkok."
media.caption: Dutch Colonial Interests

text.headline: Dutch Colonial Interests
text.text: Series of illustrations depicting Dutch colonial interests from Arbuckles' Illustrated Atlas of Fifty Principal Nations of The World.

media.alt: A vintage car is parked on a street, with a sign on the side reading "Native Daughters of Spokane, Born Before the Fire." Two women in hats sit in the back of the car, one holding an umbrella, while a man in the front appears to be driving. In the background is a building with a sign that says "Big Bend Restaurant" and another advertising a laundry service. The awnings over the windows have stripes.
media.caption: Born Before the Fire

text.headline: Born Before the Fire
text.text: Two women riding in the back of an automobile in a parade line, flying a banner reading "Native Daughters of Spokane. Born Before the Fire."

media.alt: A wide street lined with wooden buildings stretches into the distance. On the left, a building with "OK" written on it stands beside a horse-drawn carriage. Utility poles and lampposts are visible along the sidewalks. In the background, there are trees and hills.
media.caption: Colville, Washington

text.headline: Colville, Washington
text.text: Small, sparsely populated city center of Colville, WA.

media.alt: A group of people dressed in costumes stand on a float shaped like a boat with circular shields. The float has a sign that reads "BOUND FOR WINELAND" in bold letters. The background shows buildings with large windows, and there are onlookers nearby.
media.caption: Bound for Wineland

text.headline: Bound for Wineland
text.text: Charles Evans as Leif Eriksson in “Bound for Wineland” parade.

media.alt: A panoramic view of a town in a valley surrounded by hills and mountains. There are numerous buildings, both large and small, with roads lined with trees. In the foreground, three figures stand on rock formations overlooking the town. The landscape is dotted with additional trees, and there is a mix of open fields and structures.
media.caption: Birds Eye View of Spokane

text.headline: Birds Eye View of Spokane
text.text: Illustration of Spokane created in the early 1880s.

media.alt: Two men standing on a rocky outcrop, one pointing, with a few houses and trees in the background. On an adjacent rock, a person sits under an umbrella next to an easel. More houses and a path are visible in the distance.
media.caption: Birds Eye View of Spokane, Detail

text.headline: Birds Eye View of Spokane, Detail
text.text: Detail of illustration, revealing the artist beside two figures wearing top hats.

media.alt: A triangular emblem with a red interior and a black border, containing a large letter "S" at the center. Above the triangle, the text reads "MADE IN SPOKANE." Below the triangle is a small oval design with text indicating "ALLIED PRINTING TRADES UNION LABEL COUNCIL SPOKANE, WASH."
media.caption: Made in Spokane

text.headline: Made in Spokane
text.text: Letterhead logo for the 1500, 000 Club from an invoice issued to the Spokane Flour Mills.
